institutionalProfile.parentUniversity: Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University - Gurajada Vizianagaram
institutionalProfile.approvals: Approved by Pharmacy Council of India (PCI), New Delhi
institutionalProfile.hostel: Residential Facilitation for Male & Female Students
institutionalProfile.about: Established in 2025, the JNTU-GV College of Pharmaceutical Sciences, Vizianagaram, stands as a premier constituent institution dedicated to advancing pharmaceutical education, interdisciplinary research, and clinical excellence. We are committed to nurturing ethically grounded and professionally competent pharmaceutical scientists for the global healthcare landscape.
institutionalProfile.vision: To emerge as a global epicenter for pharmaceutical excellence, fostering innovation, superior pedagogical standards, and high-impact research for the betterment of society.
institutionalProfile.mission: To deliver a comprehensive and evolving curriculum in pharmaceutical sciences that integrates theoretical rigor with practical proficiency; to spearhead research in drug discovery and healthcare technologies; to cultivate strategic industry-academia partnerships; and to ensure the holistic professional development of our student community.

academicPrograms.name: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharm)
academicPrograms.note: Admission to the undergraduate program is strictly regulated through EAPCET rankings in accordance with the Council and University statutes.

governanceFramework.title: Institutional Constituent Status
governanceFramework.description: Recognized as a premier constituent college of JNTU-GV, operating under the regulatory oversight of PCI, New Delhi.

governanceFramework.title: Admissions & Statutory Compliance
governanceFramework.description: Admissions are merit-based, determined by EAPCET rankings and adherence to the State Council of Higher Education guidelines.

governanceFramework.title: Infrastructure & Research Facilities
governanceFramework.description: State-of-the-art laboratory infrastructure, specialized research wings, and residential facilities designed to foster an academic ecosystem.

governanceFramework.title: Governance & Transparency
governanceFramework.description: Committed to public accountability through the publication of mandatory disclosures, administration structures, and academic frameworks.

mandatoryDisclosures.title: 1. Institutional Identity & Accreditation
items: Official nomenclature and university affiliation
Constituency status under the JNTU-GV University Act
PCI Approval and Sanctioned Intake documentation
Contact coordinates for administrative offices

mandatoryDisclosures.title: 2. Regulatory Approvals & Certifications
items: Pharmacy Council of India (PCI) Approval Letter [2025-26] (/downloads/JNTU-GV PCI APPROVAL 2025-26.pdf)
University Affiliation & Constituent Orders
EAPCET Counselling Code: JNVPSF
Statutory compliance for sanctioned student intake

mandatoryDisclosures.title: 3. University Leadership & Administration
items: Hon'ble Chancellor: Shri Justice(Retd.) S. Abdul Nazeer
Hon'ble Vice Chancellor: Prof. V V Subba Rao
Registrar: Prof. D. Rajyalakshmi
Institutional Governing Body and Academic Council

mandatoryDisclosures.title: 4. Academic Curriculum & Framework
items: Comprehensive B.Pharmacy syllabus as per PCI norms
Eligibility criteria for undergraduate and lateral entry admissions
Evaluation methodology and academic calendar
Industry-integrated learning modules and internships

mandatoryDisclosures.title: 5. Faculty Excellence & Research Repository
items: Distinguished faculty roster with specialized expertise
Publication records and research focus areas
Faculty development programs and academic appointments
Administrative roles and departmental responsibilities

mandatoryDisclosures.title: 6. Campus Amenities & Infrastructure
items: Advanced instrumentation and formulation laboratories
Digital library repository and ICT-enabled classrooms
Boys' and Girls' residential facilities within the campus
Student health, wellness, and transport services

studentWelfareSupport.title: Anti-Ragging Vigilance
studentWelfareSupport.description: A zero-tolerance policy environment supported by a statutory committee, ensuring a safe and inclusive campus for all scholars.

studentWelfareSupport.title: Ombudsman & Grievance Redressal
studentWelfareSupport.description: A formalized mechanism for the resolution of academic and administrative concerns for students, faculty, and stakeholders.

studentWelfareSupport.title: Residential Life & Support
studentWelfareSupport.description: In-campus residential facilities for boys and girls, governed by strict codes of conduct and student wellbeing protocols.

studentWelfareSupport.title: Professional Development & Placement
studentWelfareSupport.description: Strategic initiatives to bridge industry-academia gaps, facilitating internships and career opportunities in the global pharmacy sector.

institutionalNotices.title: B.Pharmacy Admissions Open
institutionalNotices.summary: Prospective students are invited to apply for the B.Pharmacy program. Admissions are processed via EAPCET counseling protocols.

institutionalNotices.title: Faculty Appointment Walk-in Interviews
institutionalNotices.summary: The institution is recruiting specialized faculty for pharmaceutical sciences. Please refer to the official notification for criteria.

institutionalNotices.title: Residential Hall Accreditations
institutionalNotices.summary: Official confirmation of the operational readiness of boys' and girls' residential halls for the current academic year.

facultyRepository.name: Dr. K. Atchuta Kumar
facultyRepository.experience: 19 years in Pedagogical Excellence & Pharmacy Research
facultyRepository.profile: Dr. K. Atchuta Kumar leads the institution with a distinguished record in pharmaceutical education and research, steering the college towards academic and clinical prominence.

facultyRepository.name: Dr. Swathi Nageswara
facultyRepository.profile: Dr. Swathi Nageswara specializes in advanced pharmaceutical studies, contributing significantly to the academic growth and research output of the department.

facultyRepository.name: Mr. Sheik Jakir Hussain Mustaq
facultyRepository.profile: Mr. Sheik Jakir Hussain Mustaq integrates industrial insights with academic rigor, preparing students for professional excellence in the pharmaceutical sector.

facultyRepository.name: Mrs. G. Vasavi
facultyRepository.profile: Mrs. G. Vasavi is dedicated to student mentorship and instructional excellence, focusing on the core foundations of pharmaceutical sciences.

principalsMessage.title: Principal's Desk
principalsMessage.message: The JNTU-GV College of Pharmaceutical Sciences is dedicated to the pursuit of excellence in pharmacy education, fostering an environment of academic discipline and research innovation. Our mission is to cultivate a generation of healthcare professionals who are not only technically proficient but also ethically committed to the advancement of human health.
